surface_verification.justification: Isolated single-process stack (built client + SHIPWRIGHT_STATIC_DIR + tsx server on 127.0.0.1:3947, temp USERPROFILE). 1 spec PASSED in real Chromium: a tab refocus on a live terminal WS emits a {type:ping} probe frame (tx) and the server replies {type:pong} (rx); the healthy socket is NOT closed (data-ws-ready stays true). The full sleep/Tailscale partition-recovery is not deterministically E2E-reproducible (no real network partition in Playwright) — it is covered by unit tests on both ends + a manual post-deploy smoke (sleep/return).

behaviors.behavior: AC-1: an open socket emits a {type:ping} heartbeat every interval
AC-2: any inbound message resets the missed-pong run (no reap while alive)
behaviors.evidence: client useTerminalSocket.heartbeat.test AC-2 + wsHeartbeat.test createWsHeartbeatMonitor notePong

behaviors.behavior: AC-3: a silently-dead OPEN socket is reaped after MAX_MISSED intervals and reconnects
AC-4: focus / pageshow / visibilitychange each re-arm an exhausted budget + reconnect
behaviors.evidence: client useTerminalSocket.heartbeat.test AC-4 (parameterized over all 3 triggers)

behaviors.behavior: AC-4d: a refocus while a reconnect is CONNECTING does NOT double-connect (orphan guard)
AC-5: an eager refocus probe closes an OPEN-but-dead socket; AC-5b a socket that answers is kept
AC-5c: a stale probe timer does not close a healthy reconnected socket (instance-bound)
behaviors.evidence: client useTerminalSocket.heartbeat.test AC-5c (review MED regression guard)

behaviors.behavior: AC-6: a refocus NEVER reconnects a replay-only (done) attach
behaviors.evidence: client useTerminalSocket.heartbeat.test AC-6 + existing replay-only one-shot test

behaviors.behavior: AC-7: server replies {type:pong} to {type:ping} BEFORE the role gate (writer / reader / roleless), no pty side-effect
End-to-end wire: a real-browser tab refocus on a live terminal WS yields ping(tx)->pong(rx), socket stays open
Full recovery after a real OS-sleep / Tailscale partition (socket silently dead, browser fires no close event)
